meta.title: BST Force and Boundary Layer Data
meta.description: Three geometric layers of D_IV^5, each carrying one force and one boundary condition, plus the entropy/Godel information layer.

force.name: Electromagnetism
force.mediator_description: Phase oscillation on S^1 with zero winding number
force.mechanism: Phase gradient on the contact graph; Maxwell equations are geometric consequences of S^1 phase oscillation

boundary_condition.name: Electric charge quantization
boundary_condition.description: Winding number n on S^1 is integer-valued. Charge = n/3 for quarks (Z_3 subgroup), integer for leptons.

force.name: Strong force (QCD)
force.mediator_description: Z_3 partial winding mediator on CP^2, carries color charge
force.mechanism: Z_3 closure requirement on CP^2; quarks are partial circuits that must complete; confinement is topological, not dynamical

boundary_condition.name: Color confinement (Z_3 closure)
boundary_condition.description: Every physical state must be a complete Z_3 circuit on CP^2. Isolated quarks are non-states — open circuits. Baryons (3 quarks) and mesons (quark-antiquark) are the allowed closures.

layers.name: Hopf Fibration S^3 -> S^2
layers.geometry: Hopf fiber (S^3 total space, S^2 base, S^1 fiber)

force.name: Weak force
force.mediator_description: Excitations of the Hopf fibration S^3 -> S^2; massive because Hopf geometry costs energy to excite
force.mechanism: Topology oscillations on the Hopf fiber mediate flavor change; Weinberg angle is the mixing ratio between S^1 phase (EM) and Hopf fiber (weak)

boundary_condition.name: Chirality (left-handed weak coupling)
boundary_condition.description: The Hopf fibration is intrinsically chiral — geometry is right-handed, so the weak force couples only to left-handed fermions. This is geometric, not imposed.

layers.name: Bulk D_IV^5 Geometry
layers.role: Gravity as emergent curvature of the contact graph

force.name: Gravity
force.mediator: Contact graph curvature (no graviton in BST)
force.mediator_description: Gravity is not mediated by a particle; it is the curvature of the contact graph itself, emergent from the Bergman metric on D_IV^5
force.mechanism: Mass curves the contact graph; the hierarchy m_e/m_Pl = alpha^6 is geometric (exponent = n_C + 1). Gravity is weak because it is a 24th-order effect in alpha.

boundary_condition.name: Equivalence principle
boundary_condition.description: All objects fall the same way because gravity IS the contact graph geometry — everything lives on the same graph. The equivalence principle is not a postulate but a tautology.

layers.name: Information / Observer Layer
layers.role: Entropy production, Godel self-knowledge limit, observer physics

force.name: Entropy (thermodynamic arrow)
force.mediator_description: Each contact commitment is irreversible — the append-only log grows monotonically. This IS the arrow of time.
force.mechanism: Entropy is force (Casey's Principle). The fill fraction f = 19.1% is the maximum self-knowledge any observer can achieve — the Godel limit applied to physics.

boundary_condition.name: Godel self-knowledge limit
boundary_condition.description: No observer within D_IV^5 can know more than 19.1% of its own state. Lambda*N = 9/5 is the reality budget. Entropy = force, Godel = boundary (Casey's Principle, T315).

standard_model_mapping.description: How BST geometric layers map to Standard Model gauge groups

gauge_hierarchy_reading.description: Seeley-DeWitt heat kernel speaking pairs read off SM gauge groups at specific levels k

caseys_principle.statement: Entropy = force = counting (depth 0). Godel = boundary = definition (depth 0). Force + boundary = directed evolution.
caseys_principle.description: The universe's entire program in two words: force (entropy drives change) and boundary (Godel limits knowledge). Together they produce directed evolution — the universe computing itself within its own limits.
